2 weeks status post L4-5 laminectomy and decompression on 2/2/2026 at BUMCP with Dr.
Patient is doing very well following surgery. He was discharged to a rehab facility and has
completed 2 sessions of intensive physical therapy and is now being discharged. He reports
that after surgery he had a ground-level fall and underwent a CT scan at the ER. I reviewed the
CT scan today and he has a chronic compression fracture but otherwise no acute findings.
Today patient reports that his preoperative back pain has significantly improved. He is having
postsurgical pain only. He is not taking any pain medication. On exam today patients incision
appears to be healing well. We have discussed activity restrictions during the postoperative
period. Patient will follow up with Dr. Chutkan in 4 weeks, no need for xrays at that time.

Patient presents today for follow-up. He is 2 weeks status post L4-5 laminectomy and decompression on 2/2/2026 at BUMCP with Dr. Chutkan.
Notes: General Appearance: General appearance: Alert and oriented appropriately Skin: Lumbar spine: normal and healed incision. Neurological
System: Right lower extremity sensation (location): normal. Left lower extremity sensation (location): normal. Right lower extremity motor function
(muscle group): hip flexors 5/5, quadriceps 5/5, anterior tibial 5/5, EHL 5/5, and gastrocnemius 5/5. Left lower extremity motor function (muscle
group): hip flexors 5/5, quadriceps 5/5, anterior tibial 5/5, EHL 5/5, and gastrocnemius 5/5. Lumbar Spine: Sagittal balance: normal. Midline lumbar
tenderness: none. Left lumbar tenderness lower paraspinal. ambulates with walker
